expectations.text: Exactly one new commit was created
expectations.evidence: git-log-oneline.txt shows exactly one new commit (6b6b3d9) on top of the four setup commits (7e37c48, f27059b, 2513cb0, 052b4cf).

expectations.text: The new commit IS empty — running 'git show --stat HEAD' shows zero files changed and zero insertions/deletions
expectations.evidence: commit-stat.txt (the git show --stat HEAD output) ends after the commit body with no diffstat footer at all — git shows no 'X files changed' line because the commit is genuinely empty. Working tree was clean before and after.

expectations.text: Commit was created with --allow-empty (verified by the empty diff above)
expectations.evidence: session-transcript.txt step 7 explicitly invokes 'git commit --allow-empty -m ...' and the empty diffstat in commit-stat.txt confirms the result.

expectations.text: Commit subject is 72 characters or fewer
expectations.evidence: Subject 'Investigate post-Monday-deploy API latency spike — root cause in auth middleware' is 80 characters long, exceeding the 72-char limit. The transcript at step 6 explicitly acknowledges this: 'Length check: 81 characters (over the 72 target ... this is the one place I knowingly stretched the guideline.'

expectations.text: Commit subject uses an investigation-style verb (Investigate, Trace, Diagnose, Debug, Research, Document, Identify, Analyse, Find, etc.) — NOT a code-change verb like 'Fix' or 'Add'
expectations.evidence: Subject begins with 'Investigate', a clear investigation-style verb.

expectations.text: Commit subject has no trailing period
expectations.evidence: Subject ends with 'middleware' — no trailing period.

expectations.text: Commit body has a '## Summary' section
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt line 7: '## Summary' followed by a multi-sentence summary.

expectations.text: Commit body has a '## What happened' section with multiple sentences
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt line 14: '## What happened' followed by four paragraphs of multi-sentence narrative (the session was a pure investigation; reading the file; confirmed by tailing; did not write the fix).

expectations.text: Commit body has a '## Findings' section
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt line 37: '## Findings' followed by two concrete bullets.

expectations.text: Commit body mentions 'src/middleware/auth.ts' (the user's exact file reference is preserved)
expectations.evidence: Appears verbatim in 'What happened' (line 20), 'Findings' (line 38), and 'State / Blocked' (line 57) sections.

expectations.text: Commit body mentions 'validateSession' (the user's exact function reference is preserved)
expectations.evidence: Appears as 'validateSession()' in 'What happened' (line 21), 'Findings' (line 38), and 'State / Blocked' (line 57).

expectations.text: Commit body mentions 'line 23' or 'around line 23' (the user's exact location reference is preserved)
expectations.evidence: Appears as 'around line 23' in 'What happened' (line 20), 'Findings' (line 38), and 'State / Blocked' (line 57).

expectations.text: Commit body mentions postgres, query, cache, OR slow query log (the trace evidence)
expectations.evidence: All four are present: 'Postgres' (lines 9, 23, 27, 39, 58), 'session cache' / 'cache check' (lines 10, 21, 39, 56), 'slow query log' (lines 27, 41, 49), 'query' (multiple).

expectations.text: Commit body total length is at least 500 characters (empty commit needs MORE body, not less)
expectations.evidence: Total commit-full.txt is 2779 characters; the body alone (## Summary through Co-Authored-By trailer) is roughly 2500 characters — well over the 500-char minimum.
Final line of body (line 61): 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>'.

expectations.text: Working tree is clean after the commit (unchanged from start)
expectations.evidence: git-status.txt: 'On branch main / nothing to commit, working tree clean'.
Transcript step 7 compliance check: 'No git push. Confirmed — I never ran push.' No push command appears anywhere in the transcript.

Transcript shows only outputs/ files written (commit-full.txt, commit-stat.txt, git-log-oneline.txt, git-status.txt, session-transcript.txt). No logs/ directory mentioned. Working tree is clean per git-status.txt.

expectations.text: No JSON checkpoint files were created
expectations.evidence: Transcript Phase 4 was explicitly skipped per the empty-tree skip rule: '.claude/settings.json was not created or modified'. No JSON files written. Working tree confirmed clean.
